exemplar.title: Analysis of the Influence of Lionel Messi on FC Barcelona's Performance Metrics

detailedExemplarData.title: Analysis of the Influence of Lionel Messi on FC Barcelona's Performance Metrics

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear overall structure with a table of contents, numbered headings and consistent pagination
Recognisable logical sequence (rationale â data â analysis â evaluation)
Graphs and tables are generally grouped meaningfully within appropriate sections
weaknesses: Frequent lapses in logic (mis-typed GPM formula, omitted intermediate values) interrupt coherence
Unnecessary repetition (definitions appear multiple times) and digressions reduce conciseness
Inconsistent formatting in headings and captions (duplicate/misplaced sub-headings, missing table titles)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report is adequately organised (band 3 for organisation) and follows a standard sequence, but coherence is only partial (band 2) due to logical gaps and omitted details. Conciseness suffers from repetitions and irrelevant material, placing it at band 2 overall. A score of 2/4 reflects these mixed strengths and weaknesses.

strengths: Use of appropriate mathematical notation (Î£, Î¼, Ï, RÂ²) in many places
Effective inclusion of multiple representations: tables, bar charts, line graphs and a scatter-plot
General logical flow in explanations from definitions to interpretation
weaknesses: Inconsistent and incorrect formatting of equations (duplicated exponents, comma vs. dot decimal notation)
Missing units and variable definitions in table headers; some axes lack full labels
Key steps are skipped or misstated (pooled variance mis-copied, intermediate totals omitted), disrupting clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the student employs a variety of representations effectively (band 3) and demonstrates some command of mathematical language (band 2), inconsistent notation and gaps in explanation limit the logical clarity to band 2 overall. A 2/4 score aligns with these observations.

strengths: Clear personal motivation in choosing Messiâs performance as the topic
Evidence of personal involvement through hand-collated data and custom annotations on graphs
weaknesses: Limited independent thinking: methodology is largely drawn from standard tutorials without novel adaptation
Personal voice is intermittent and not sustained throughout
Creativity and initiative remain basic; no extension beyond prescribed statistical tools
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration shows genuine interest and some personalization (band 2 for personal approach) but limited independent thinking and minimal creativity (band 1 for both other strands). Overall, personal engagement is at band 1, warranting 1/3.

strengths: Some acknowledgement of caveats (managerial changes, data quality)
Basic evaluation remarks in conclusion on power and limitations of the analysis
weaknesses: Reflection remains surface-level, lacking depth and critical insight
No detailed analysis of how caveats would quantitatively affect results
Future implications and evaluation of outcome reliability are not rigorously explored
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is confined to generic comments and basic listing of issues (band 1 across depth, analysis and evaluation). There is no substantial or thoughtful critical reflection, so the appropriate score is 1/3.

strengths: Relevant choice of descriptive statistics, linear regression and t-test applied to raw data
Methods align well with the investigation aim and data context (band 3 relevance)
weaknesses: Frequent notation errors (mis-typed formulas for GPM, variance) and incorrect summations
Use of n instead of n-1 for variance, mis-defined degrees of freedom in the t-test
Computed values (e.g. RÂ², covariance) appear inconsistent without adequate justification
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematical methods used are appropriate for the SL course (band 3 relevance) but are applied with noticeable errors and only partial accuracy (band 2 for understanding and level appropriateness). On balance, a score of 2/6 reflects competent but error-prone use of mathematics.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations often follow a logical sequence (definition â calculation â interpretation) but key steps are skipped (total wins not shown, pooled variance mis-copied) and the variance formula is misstated.  These gaps interrupt clarity; hence the communication demonstrates âsomeâ rather than âgoodâ logical structure.

Calculations show some understanding but include several errors: incorrect GPM formula notation, mis-summed squared deviations, and an unrealistically high RÂ² given the tiny slope.  While results are not wholly invalid, accuracy is inconsistent, so understanding is partial with noticeable errors.

Tables of season data, bar charts, line graphs and a scatter-plot accompany the narrative and generally illustrate the stated metrics effectively.  Some axes lack full labels, but the variety of representations (numeric tables, algebraic formulas, graphical summaries) is appropriate and aids comprehension, meeting the descriptor for âeffective useâ.

The exploration follows a recognisable sequence (rationale â data â analysis â evaluation) and the reader can broadly follow the argument, yet numerous lapses in logic occur.  For instance, the GPM formula is mis-typed, several intermediate values are omitted (total wins for the Messi era are only given later) and variance is incorrectly defined in two different ways.  These discontinuities interrupt the flow and show that coherence is only partial rather than sustained.

The exploration shows some initiative in gathering multi-season data and combining several statistical tools, yet the mathematics itself adheres closely to textbook procedures without inventive extension (e.g., no attempt at time-series modelling or confidence intervals).  Hence only âsome creativity or initiativeâ is evident.

The student lists several plausible caveats (managerial changes, data quality) but does not analyse how each would quantitatively alter findings, nor are alternative models tested.  This constitutes only basic critical analysis.

Outcome evaluation is confined to stating that the statistical evidence is âcompellingâ and suggesting future studies could add xG.  There is no rigorous assessment of the reliability of the numerical results or exploration of future implications, so evaluation remains limited.

Reflection is restricted mainly to a short evaluation section and generic comments in the conclusion about the power and limitations of mathematics.  These remarks are surface-level and do not probe the implications of specific numerical results, indicating limited rather than meaningful reflection.

The student uses mathematical symbols (Î£, Î¼, Ï, RÂ²) and equations, but many are formatted incorrectly or include typographical errors (e.g. duplicated exponents in the GPM formula, commas used for decimals, misplaced subscripts).  These inconsistencies show only partial command of mathematical language.

The selection of a beloved football topic, hand-collated data and personalised commentary (stars marking seasons on graphs) provide clear evidence that the investigation is framed from the studentâs own perspective.  However, the personal voice is intermittent, preventing the work from reaching a highly personal or innovative level.

At 24 pages the report contains several repetitions (definitions of GPM, win %, variance appear twice) and long verbatim quotations from internet sources that do not advance the analysis.  While most content is on topic, these superfluous elements and digressions about football tactics reduce conciseness, so the work is only âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elementsâ.

Descriptive statistics, regression and a two-sample t-test are all pertinent choices for comparing eras and assessing association, satisfying the descriptor for ârelevant mathematics used appropriatelyâ.  The selection aligns with the investigation aim and is applied to raw data rather than illustrative examples only.

The idea to study Messiâs influence shows personal interest, yet most of the mathematical approach (descriptive stats, linear regression, t-test) is taken straight from standard tutorials with no novel adaptation.  Independent decisions (e.g., why three eras were delineated as they were) are asserted rather than justified, so evidence of independent thinking remains limited.

A clear table of contents, numbered headings and sub-headings, and separate introduction, body and conclusion create a solid structural backbone.  Pagination is consistent and graphs/tables are grouped in the appropriate sections.  Although some headings are duplicated or misplaced (e.g. Methodology lists âAssumptionsâ as point 3), overall organisation allows the reader to locate information with little difficulty.

The statistical content sits largely at SL level; no calculus, probability distributions or HL-specific techniques are employed.  Therefore the mathematics is âmostly at course levelâ but not fully commensurate with HL expectations.

annotations.comment: The title is clear and descriptive, immediately informing the reader of the explorationâs focus on Messiâs influence using performance metrics.
The table of contents shows organized structure but has inconsistent indentation and duplicate sub-bullets under section 6. Simplify formatting for clarity.
The rationale effectively conveys the studentâs personal motivation and connection to football analytics, demonstrating genuine engagement.
The background section lists useful metrics but does not justify why goal differential, win %, and GPM were chosen. Add rationale for these selections.
Listing assumptions is useful, but labelling them as minimal impact or granularity lacks justification. Clarify why these factors are considered negligible.
The data collection description should clarify whether only La Liga metrics are used or all competitions. State the scope explicitly.
Table of pre-Messi data is comprehensive but column headers lack units and variable definitions. Add footnotes or clarify each heading.
In the Messi era table, draws are formatted as \overline{4} and \mathbf{1}, which is confusing. Use consistent numeric notation (e.g. 4, 1) in LaTeX.
The table of Messiâs individual stats lacks a caption and clear context. Add a descriptive title and source reference.
The formula for GPM is mis-typed as GPM = \frac{Total\,G}^{Total\,G}^{Total\,For}. Correct notation is $$GPM=\frac{\Sigma(Goals\,For)}{\Sigma(Matches)}$$.
Decimal commas (2,04) are used inconsistently with dot notation in equations. Standardize to one format (e.g. 2.04) throughout.
The Win% formula labels âMatches Playerâ incorrectly and omits definition of variables. Use $$Win\%=\frac{Total\,Wins}{Total\,Matches}\times100$$ and define symbols.
The inline equation for goal differential is merged into a heading and isnât clearly separated. Place $$GoalDifferential=GoalsFor-GoalsAgainst$$ on its own line.
The variance formula omits a closing parenthesis and uses $n$ rather than $n-1$ for a sample. Correct to $$\frac{\sum(X_i-\mu)^2}{n-1}$$.
The formula for Xi is clearly presented but could use summation notation to reinforce general use. Consider $$X_i=\frac{Goals_i}{Matches_i}$$.
The numeric example uses comma formatting â2,105â then â2,1â without explaining rounding rules. State precision and rounding method.
The variance formula on page 15 uses \left(\sum(X_i-Î¼)\right)^2 instead of summing squared deviations. Correct notation to $$\sum(X_i-Î¼)^2$$.
The detailed standard deviation calculation yields excessive decimal places. Round intermediate results (e.g. 1.25) and avoid overwhelming precision.
The regression equation $$Y=Î²_0+Î²_1X$$ is stated without discussing linearity or independence assumptions. Reflect on model suitability.
Covariance sum of 88.442 seems large relative to variances. Check computations and explain how this value was obtained.
The definition $$R^2=\frac{Explained\,Variance}{Total\,Variance}$$ is generic. Specify SSR and SST terms to clarify what is explained vs total.
Hypothesis testing section omits normality of GPM distributions and equal variance assumptions. Discuss how these conditions are met or justified.
The t-statistic formula uses $n_z+n_z-2$ instead of $n_1+n_2-2$ in df expression. Correct notation and clarify subscripts.
In the interpretation, causal language implies Messi alone drove performance. Acknowledge correlation vs. causation and contextual factors.
